content.full_text:
May 12, 1992

Carla Wardlow, Acting Division Chief  
Technical Assistance and Analysis Division  
Fair Political Practices Commission  
428 J Street, Suite 800  
P.O. Box 807  
Sacramento, California 95804-0804

Dear Ms. Wardlow:

Re: Campaign Statement Filing Obligation  
Carol Rowen, Candidate  
Special Primary Election - 20th Senatorial District  
Covering Period: 01/01/92 to 03/22/92

This is to request confirmation of telephone advice provided to this office by Maryann Kvasager of your staff concerning campaign statement filing obligations for Carol Rowen.

The situation in question is as follows:

1. Ms. Rowen paid a candidate filing fee of $525 on February 20 for the office of State Senator, 20th District.

2. This office received a Statement of Organization for the Friends of Carol Rowen for State Senator Committee. The statement indicated the committee qualified on February 25.

3. The deadline to file the 1st pre-election campaign statement was February 27. This filing period covered January 1 thru February 22.

This office on March 9 mailed to Ms. Rowen a written notice regarding her failure to file a campaign statement for this period.

4. On March 27 a Form 490 campaign statement was filed on behalf of Ms. Rowen's committee covering the 2nd filing period ending on March 21. Although the statement was submitted for the 2nd filing, the period covered was January 1 through March 21.

The question of whether Ms. Rowen had a legal filing obligation for the 1st reporting period was discussed numerous times with staff members from the Secretary of State's office and your office. On May 1, Ms. Kvasager advised this office by telephone that the commission had agreed with the Secretary of State's decision of accepting the March 27 statement as a 1st and 2nd pre-election combined statement, nullifying any penalties.

In review of this advice, this office is requesting written confirmation of this decision. Copies of any materials on which you based your decision would be appreciated. If your advice was based on interpretation of current laws, please let me know.

Thank you for your assistance and please call me if you have any questions.

Very truly yours,

CHARLES WEISSBURD
Registrar-Recorder/County Clerk

STELLA MATTHEWS, Head
Campaign Reporting Section
